headline: The intertwined stories of Putin and Stalin's refugees in a literary circle near Paris
description: In the Paris suburb of Vanves, a small literary circle keeps alive the memory of the great Russian poet Marina Tsvetaeva, in the apartment where she lived before her doomed return to Stalin's Russia. Today's Russian dissidents see in her verses a reflection of their own inner turmoil.

headline: Gulf monarchies divided amid threats of new US strikes on Iran
Trump says cutting US troop numbers in Germany 'way down'
US airlines step up as Spirit winds down
description: Spirit Airlines, one of the first low-cost carriers in the US, had been in bankruptcy since August 2025. After last-minute talks with creditors and the White House collapsed, the carrier made a statement early Saturday to announce its immediate shutdown – leaving passengers and crew members stranded.

headline: 'In Israel, the threshold for accepting violence keeps rising'
description: The trauma following the massacres of October 7, 2023, has led to an 'uninhibited and institutionalized force' used against Palestinians and has led to the questioning of Israel's social acceptance of violence – even among liberals and pro-democracy advocates, observes sociologist Sylvaine Bulle for Le Monde.

headline: The reconstruction of George Ivanchenko, Ukrainian photographer and amputee
description: Wounded in a drone attack in the Donbas region of eastern Ukraine, young Ukrainian photographer George Ivanchenko captures intimate moments from his personal journey of recovery.

headline: 'He grabbed me by the neck': Women cyclists speak out about violence and sexual harassment
description: Amid tense encounters between drivers and cyclists in city centers and with accidents on the rise, women regularly report experiencing sexist assaults. This has even caused some to give up cycling altogether.

headline: Patrick Artus: 'AI's impact on growth will depend on redistribution policies'
Illegal rave in France draws 40,000 to 'dangerous' military site

Wales's pro-independence party hopes to top polls for first time ever

In Mali, the jihadists' goal 'is not taking Bamako, but changing the people in power'
How Paris became the capital of master sushi chefs
Why is May 1 a paid public holiday in France? The history of a hard-won celebration
description: The day celebrating worker and union movements is now celebrated in most countries around the world. In France, exceptions to the work ban on this day remain a subject of ongoing debate.

headline: Senegal's lawyers face a dilemma as new anti-LGBTQ+ law takes effect
description: Since the bill was enacted on March 30, authorities have arrested at least 100 people across the country for 'acts against nature.' Many lawyers have chosen to distance themselves from Senegalese defendants, fearing potential reprisals against themselves or their loved ones.

headline: In a small French village, a father is charged with sexual abuse of 34 victims aged 3 to 9
description: A tree, a rope, a letter. After surviving a suicide attempt, a man revealed he had committed numerous crimes against children. After a year-long and controversial investigation, he was charged, on April 10, with rape and sexual assault of minors and recording child pornography.
byline: Jérémie Pham-Lê and  Lorraine de Foucher

headline: 'Céline Dion will generate the equivalent of one-fifth of the economic impact of the Paris Olympics'
